meaning: n.多产,丰饶,肥沃,[动]产卵力,生育力,[植]结实性
examples: The species showed no significant decline in fecundity.
To be a successful replicator, there are three key characteristics: longevity, fecundity and fidelity.
Plant species dependent solely on seeds for survival or dispersal are obviously more vulnerable to any decrease in plant fecundity that occurs.
The human population exploded and without natural selection pressure, fecundity in the types economic activity flourished.
It is impossible not to be struck by the fecundity of this great 20th-century artist.
